sections.paragraph:
ὀχυρός, ά, όν (Hes. et al.; LXX; PsSol 2:1; Jos., Ant. 11, 89; TestJud 9:4; πόλεις ὀχ. Theoph. Ant. 3, 20 [p. 244, 6]) pert. to being firm/stable, strong, firm, sturdy μακροθυμία Hm 5, 2, 3.—Of firm adherence to an opinion τινὲς δὲ ἐξ αὐτῶν ἐλάλησεν ὀχυροὶ λέγοντες Lk 11:15 v.l. S. next entry.—DELG s.v. ἐχυρός.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Hm Hm = Mandates

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 746
